course_title: Clinical Skills in Psychotherapy 1: Engagement, Diagnosis, Formulation & Treatment Planning
course_description:
This course focuses on the clinical skills needed to engage with patients. In this first psychotherapy skills course you will further develop your skills in assessment of mental health issues and will develop skills in formulation and treatment planning for psychotherapy interventions. Psychotherapy Field of Study requirements will be met by this course.

This course marks an important step towards securing a good basis for effective and sensitive mental health practice. It has a focus on ensuring high quality clinical skills in assessment and treatment planning both for standard mental health care and for psychotherapy. We will also work to refine the skills and awareness necessary to develop a strong working alliance with those who seek help from you and your colleagues. You will work in weekly seminar/tutorials with an experienced clinician and tutor, and your fellow students, to strengthen the learning experience. The assessments are clinically focussed. We look forward to working with you this semester.

The seminars will examine clinical skills in building and maintaining alliance, standard mental health assessment, formulation and treatment planning as well as psychotherapy approaches (psychoanalytic, cognitive and behavioural, and systemic family therapy) to these key tasks.
They will be led by experienced clinicians and teachers with passion for clinical excellence who bring hard won skills from their own practice blended with the knowledge of theory and research in these areas.
Workshops
There will be 2 days of in-person workshops at the UQ Herston Campus, Brisbane on
12th and 13th March, 2026.
Thursday 12th March, 2026 8:30am - 4:00pm:
Examining Standard Mental Health assessment, formulation, treatment planning and therapeutic alliance
and Friday 13th March, 2026 8:30am - 4:00pm:
Examining Evidence Based Approaches to Psychotherapy Assessment, Diagnosis, Formulation and Treatment planning (systemic, psychoanalytic and CBT approaches).
Workshop leaders include
Dr Jaya Valvoi, Dr Jaime Yasky, Ms Alex Tregenza and Lisa Hardy.
You are expected to attend these workshops and if you are unable to attend you should contact Lisa Hardy to discuss. The workshops will not be recorded.
We look forward to working with you, contact us if you have any concerns.
Lisa Hardy
l.hardy@uq.edu.au
course_aims: This course aims to provide students with knowledge and skills in engagement, diagnosis, formulation and treatment planning that will support collaborative and effective mental health practice.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate knowledge and skills to engage with consumers to collaboratively address their mental health needs
Demonstrate knowledge and skills to use industry standard assessment, formulation and treatment planning approaches
Demonstrate knowledge and skills to use evidenced-based psychotherapeutic approaches to assessment, diagnosis and treatment planning.

assessment_details.title: Quiz: Mental Health Assessment and Therapeutic Alliance
assessment_details.task_description: Students will undertake a quiz (quantitative and qualitative) during the residential workshop week regarding Mental Health Assessment and Therapeutic Alliance. The information assessed will directly correlate to content delivered during the workshop day on Thursday 12th March, 2026. Criteria & Marking: Student demonstrates understanding and knowledge of the essential components, purpose and structure of a standard mental health assessment Student demonstrates knowledge of core therapeutic alliance components and the clinician’s role within collaborative mental health practice Student demonstrates the ability to reflect critically on their developing clinical practice in relation to workshop content Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Case Report: Standard Mental Health Approach
assessment_details.task_description: Case report that presents a vivid portrait of your work with a client in a MH setting. It should describe the findings of your assessment, formulation and treatment planning. Where appropriate, you should report on the process of the work you have done together and your understanding of the outcome or current state of the work. This is a formal report using a format that is appropriate to your work environment. It should demonstrate developing competence in assessment, formulation, collaborative treatment planning. 1500 words 40%. Criteria & Marking: The report describes the assessment, formulation and treatment planning that was done in collaboration with the client and, as appropriate, the report describes the process of any ongoing work and articulates changes you have made to your initial assessment. The report demonstrates developing competence in assessment, formulation, collaborative treatment planning Student has submitted a report that describes a mental health assessment process with a client. It is around 1500 words (+/- 10%) and is in formal report, appropriate to the work environment. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Case Conference Simulation: Student Oral Presentation
assessment_details.due_date: 21/05/2026 - 28/05/2026 Student presentations will occur during standard tutorial time. There is a possibility that the tutorial time may be extended, depending on number of student presentations to be completed across the 2 weeks. Student presentations are recorded for marking purposes only.
assessment_details.task_description: Students are to present a (de-identified) thoughtful 10 - 15 minute specialist case formulation of their work with a client or family in their workplace. The P's formulation model should be utilised, incorporating one of the psychotherapy approaches studied in the course i.e. psychoanalytic, cognitive, behavioural, and systemic. A preliminary treatment plan and brief rationale should be presented. Students must engage in a 5-10 minute Q&A time after their presentation to further engage in clarification of content or answer any questions, simulating a case conference environment in a public mental health setting. Student presentations are recorded for marking purposes only. 40%. Criteria & Marking: Student presentation that describes the assessment of an individual or family, appropriate to a work environment with the intention of simulating a case conference presentation and treatment progress update. As appropriate, the presentation describes the psychotherapy focussed assessment, formulation and treatment planning that was done in collaboration with the client/s. The presentation along with Q&A component clearly demonstrates developing competence in psychotherapy assessment, formulation, collaborative treatment planning. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.topic: Introduction to Clinical Skills in Psychotherapy 1 (PXMH7111) & Beginning to Explore The Therapeutic Alliance An overview of PXMH 7111 and introductions. Beginning to explore Therapeutic Alliance
Diagnosis in Mental Health The role of therapeutic alliance in practice.

Clinical Skills in Mental Health Assessment & Formulation - Residential Workshops Mental health assessment, assessment for psychotherapy and skills for building working alliance PLEASE NOTE - There is no Thursday night tutorial on 12th March 2026 (workshop week).
Foundations of Case Formulation Skills in standard mental health assessment, formulation and treatment planning
Comorbidity in Mental Health Standard mental health assessment, formulation and treatment planning ASSESSMENT 1 Due Thursday 27th March, 2025 13:00hra.

Working with Anxiety Skills in standard mental health assessment. In this tutorial there will also be a focus on formulation from a psychoanalytic approach when working with anxiety and short client case example. ASSESSMENT 1 Due Thursday 2rd APRIL 2026 21:00hrs Easter Public Holidays 3rd-6th April 2026 In Semester Break 6th April-13th April 2026

OCD & Depression Integration of learnings regarding standard mental health assessment. This tutorial will also focus on CBT formulation and brief client example when working with clients who have OCD or depression.
Mental State Examination Assessment, formulation and treatment planning in psychoanalytic therapy MID SEMESTER BREAK FOLLOWS - NO TUTORIAL 24th April, 2025
Cultural Formulation & The Australian Context Assessment, formulation and treatment planning in cognitive and behavioural therapy ASSESSMENT 2 Due Friday 1st May 2026 21:00hrs.

Family & Systemic Formulation Assessment, formulation and treatment planning in systemic family therapy - Lisa Hardy and Alex Tregenza After reading discussion this tutorial will apply systemic formulation principles to a client case study example.

Tutorial practical class: practicing formulation through specialist approaches This tutorial will be a practical session that will focus on a client case example. The group will think through how the formulation may be approached from the 3 specialist approaches of systemic, CBT or Psychoanalytic perspectives.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 12 To Week 13 (18 May - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Case Conference Simulation: Student Oral Presentation Prepare a 10 minute oral presentation of a client formulation using the P's, written from one of the specialist approaches (Systemic, CBT or Psychoanalytic) as well as a preliminary treatment plan. There will be a 5 minute Q&A time following presentation.

Case Conference Simulation: Student Oral Presentation Prepare a 10 minute oral presentation of a client formulation using the P's, written from one of the specialist approaches (Systemic, CBT or Psychoanalytic) as well as a preliminary treatment plan. There will be a 5 minute Q&A time following presentation.
